name: chokepoint-web-shells
bodyMarkdown:
# Web Shell Persistence — Detection Chokepoint

**Priority:** CRITICAL | **Tactic:** Persistence, Initial Access, Execution | **Difficulty:** MEDIUM | **Prevalence:** HIGH
**Techniques:** T1505.003, T1190, T1059.004

## Overview
Web shells persist via HTTP. Invariant: web server process must spawn child OS interpreter to execute commands — parent-child relationship is kernel-observable regardless of shell language.

## Chokepoint Stages

### 1. Shell Deployment
Must deploy executable code reachable by web server.
Observable: EID 11 (script written to web root), web server access logs.

### 2. Shell Execution
HTTP request causes web server to spawn child interpreter.
Observable: EID 1 (w3wp.exe/httpd spawning cmd.exe/powershell.exe/bash).

### 3. Command Execution
Spawned interpreter executes recon/lateral movement/exfil.
Observable: EID 1 (CommandLine content), EID 3 (network from child).

## Variations (11 tracked)
China Chopper, Godzilla, Behinder, AntSword, Neo-reGeorg, LEMURLOOT (MOVEit), GLASSTOKEN (Ivanti), SALTWATER (Barracuda), Fileless IIS Modules, Polyglot/Steganographic, SSTI.

## Detection Logic
- **Research:** Any web server child process creation
- **Hunt:** EID 11 (script in web path) + EID 1 (cmd.exe/powershell.exe child of web server)
- **Analyst:** EID 11 + EID 1 (-enc/recon commands) + EID 3 (outbound from child)
